name: Quebec City
summary: Quebec City is a French-speaking storybook town on a cliff above the wide St. Lawrence River. It is the only walled city in North America north of Mexico, with cobbled lanes and the castle-like Chateau Frontenac at its heart.
funFacts: Quebec City still has its full stone walls, gates, and cannons, making it the only walled city left in North America north of Mexico.
The Chateau Frontenac has copper roofs and pointy turrets like a real castle, and is said to be the most photographed hotel on Earth.
Montmorency Falls drops 83 metres, about 30 metres taller than Niagara Falls, and you can ride a cable car or zoom across on a zipline.
Quebec City was founded in 1608 by French explorer Samuel de Champlain, making it more than 400 years old and the heart of New France.
Each winter a giant snowman named Bonhomme Carnaval is handed the keys to the city to start a huge ice and snow festival.
thingsToDo: Walk along the old stone ramparts and through the gates of Old Quebec, spotting cannons left over from 1700s battles.
Ride the funicular cable railway between Lower Town and Upper Town to reach the Chateau Frontenac at the top.
Explore Place Royale in Lower Town, where Champlain set up his trading post in 1608, and find the giant painted history mural.
Wander the colourful little shops and flower-lined cobbles of the Petit-Champlain quarter, one of North America's oldest streets.
Run around the Plains of Abraham battlefield park with picnic lawns, big maple trees and a museum full of treasure hunts.
Take the cable car up Montmorency Falls and walk across the suspension footbridge right above the roaring water.

wildlife.name: Beluga Whale
wildlife.description: Friendly snow-white whale that lives all year in the cold St. Lawrence River.

wildlife.name: Beaver
wildlife.description: Canada's national animal, a furry rodent that builds wooden dams with its flat tail.

wildlife.name: Snowy Owl
wildlife.description: Big white owl, the official bird of Quebec, that hunts across snowy fields in winter.

wildlife.name: Canada Goose
wildlife.description: Black-headed goose with a white chinstrap that flies in V-shapes honking loudly.

localWord.meaning: Hello, the friendly French greeting you will hear everywhere in Quebec.

images.alt: The grand green-roofed Chateau Frontenac hotel towers over the rooftops of Quebec City beside the wide St. Lawrence River.
The star-shaped stone fortress walls of the Citadelle, part of Old Quebec's UNESCO walled city, rise above the St. Lawrence under a blue sky.
Old stone houses with steep roofs glow under warm lights around Place Royale square at night in Old Quebec.
A narrow cobbled lane in the Petit-Champlain quarter is lined with colourful little shops and flower boxes.
The wide grassy lawns of the Plains of Abraham battlefield park in Quebec City on a sunny day.
A huge curtain of white water plunges down the tall cliff at Montmorency Falls just outside Quebec City.
A snow-white beluga whale glides through the water with its rounded forehead poking up.
A North American beaver sits in the sun on a log at Six Mile Lake Provincial Park, Ontario.
A snowy owl with bright yellow eyes stands on the snow.
Close-up of a Canada goose with a black head and the famous white chinstrap.
